Uses computer vision to automatically classify, detect, count, and measure areas, linears, and objects from digital plans. Now available within both OnScreen Takeoff and PlanSwift — PlanSwift with Takeoff Boost released May 6, 2026, extending AI-powered Auto Takeoff, Auto Count, Auto Scale, and Auto Bookmark to PlanSwift users.
tools.pricing: Custom quote; included for ConstructConnect platform subscribers
tools.results: Cuts takeoff hours significantly (company-reported). Available across ConstructConnect's large estimating customer base via both OnScreen Takeoff and PlanSwift.
tools.integrations: ConstructConnect OnScreen Takeoff (live); PlanSwift with Takeoff Boost (live as of May 6, 2026)
tools.spoken: ConstructConnect Takeoff Boost is an AI-powered takeoff service built on Google Cloud using Gemini and NVIDIA computer vision. It automatically classifies, detects, counts, and measures areas, linears, and objects directly from digital plan sets and converts them into structured measurements in seconds. Available now to ConstructConnect customers through OnScreen Takeoff with PlanSwift integration coming. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: Drawer AI
tools.description: AI-powered automated electrical takeoff and estimating platform purpose-built for electrical subcontractors and estimators. Reads PDF blueprints to automatically detect and quantify all electrical devices, fixtures, and panels, and generates branch circuit routing with automatic wire sizing and voltage drop calculations. Merges multiple drawing files into a single layout and automatically associates symbols with schedules. Launched in 2024 with a $5M seed investment from Brick & Mortar Ventures and Base-10 Partners. Pricing: Starter $150/user/month; Pro $300/user/month; Enterprise custom.
tools.pricing: Starter $150/user/month; Pro $300/user/month; Enterprise custom; 15-day money-back guarantee
tools.results: Starr Electric Company: analyzed 2,600+ fixtures and 3,400+ power devices with 70% time savings. Platform reduces electrical takeoff time by approximately 70% with 10x ROI claim. $5M seed funding (Brick & Mortar Ventures, Base-10 Partners).
tools.integrations: Standalone platform; PDF blueprint import; Excel and PDF export
tools.spoken: Drawer AI is a purpose-built electrical takeoff and estimating platform for electrical subcontractors and estimators. It reads PDF blueprints to detect and quantify all electrical devices, fixtures, and panels, generates branch circuit routing with automatic wire sizing, and merges multiple drawing files into a single layout. Backed by five million dollars in seed funding from Brick and Mortar Ventures and Base-Ten Partners. Starr Electric Company analyzed twenty-six hundred fixtures and thirty-four hundred power devices with a seventy percent time savings using the platform. Pricing starts at one hundred fifty dollars per user per month.

tools.name: TaksoAi
tools.description: AI takeoff platform for HVAC, mechanical, and plumbing specialty contractors. Recognizes 60+ pipe and HVAC fittings including elbows, reducers, and valves. Next-Gen Duct Algorithm V2.0 for duct and pipe sizing. Most HVAC plans process in under 15 minutes. New strategic integration with Wendes Systems (a veteran 64-bit HVAC and mechanical estimating platform) announced, embedding TaksoAi's AI takeoff directly into Wendes estimating workflows.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: Processes most mechanical plans in under 15 minutes; identifies 38+ pipe and plumbing fitting types with high accuracy; purpose-built for mechanical, HVAC, and piping contractors.
tools.integrations: Wendes Systems (direct integration announced 2025-2026), Excel/CSV export for use with other HVAC estimating software
tools.spoken: TaksoAi is an AI takeoff platform purpose-built for mechanical, HVAC, piping, and plumbing subcontractors. Founded in Toronto in 2021, it runs a proprietary AI engine that identifies thirty-eight or more pipe and plumbing fitting types, processing most mechanical plans in under fifteen minutes. Its SpecTool feature automates specification analysis so estimators can instantly query parameters like pressure classes across an entire spec document. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: XBuild
tools.description: AI-native agentic estimating platform for specialty subcontractors. Currently live for roofing contractors: ingests EagleView, GAF QuickScope, Hover, and Roofr measurement reports plus job photos and produces an editable estimate in under 15 minutes. Generates Good/Better/Best pricing with e-signature and Stripe Connect payments. Expansion roadmap targets concrete, landscaping, painting, glass and glazing, insulation, HVAC, and plumbing. Founded by former Uber and Postmates executives. Raised $19M Series A (January 2026) led by N47 with Andreessen Horowitz participating.
tools.pricing: Subscription-based with unlimited AI estimates and no per-job fees. Free trial available without credit card. Specific tier pricing not publicly disclosed.
tools.built_for: Specialty subcontractors (roofing now, expanding to MEP and other commercial trades)
tools.results: 15,000+ projects processed, $250M+ in project value, 40,000+ estimating hours saved as of January 2026 launch (company-reported).
tools.integrations: ABC Supply (real-time pricing), EagleView, Hover, Roofr, GAF QuickScope measurement platforms. No Procore or Autodesk integration at launch.

tools.name: Trimble LiveCount AI Automate Takeoff
tools.description: AI-powered automated takeoff capability within Trimble's LiveCount platform (part of Accubid Classic and Accubid Anywhere) and Estimation MEP Pro, targeting electrical and MEP specialty subcontractors. Automatically detects and counts outlets, switches, fire alarm systems, and other electrical and MEP devices from PDF drawings without manual marking. Auto-detects and sets drawing scale at a 95% success rate. Has identified, classified, and counted 200,000+ objects monthly across the user base, saving 3,500+ annual estimating hours.
tools.pricing: Included in existing Trimble Accubid subscriptions; no separate AI pricing disclosed.
tools.results: 95% drawing scale auto-detection rate. 200,000+ objects classified monthly. 3,500+ estimating hours saved annually across the user base (Trimble-reported).
tools.integrations: Trimble Construction One ecosystem, Autodesk Revit via Trimble Connect, Sage via Trimble Estimation Desktop

tools.name: Quotr.ai
tools.description: All-in-one AI construction estimating, takeoff, and procurement platform from a Berkeley SkyDeck-backed startup (Batch 19). Uploads PDF plans or Revit model data, auto-detects rooms, counts, openings, and symbols with estimator control, and applies built-in item libraries or custom labor and assembly rules with location-based pricing. Claims 90% faster takeoffs and 95% accuracy. Includes procurement workflows alongside estimating.
tools.pricing: Monthly platform plans with a 7-day free trial. Per-project analysis pricing also available. Specific tier pricing not prominently disclosed.
tools.results: Vanderbilt University case study published. Claims 40-50% material savings.
tools.integrations: Autodesk Revit (native input). No confirmed Procore or Sage integrations.

tools.name: Space AI
tools.description: AI-powered construction bidding and project management platform founded in 2025. Combines bid invitation management, subcontractor database, AI bid-competitiveness scoring, scope gap analysis, and award tracking in a single interface. Free tier covers basic bidding on limited projects; the Professional tier adds unlimited bidding and AI analysis. Self-reports use by 500+ construction teams and a 23% reduction in project delays.
tools.pricing: Free tier (limited projects); Professional at $99/user/month; Enterprise with volume discounts
tools.results: 500+ construction teams reported. Claims 23% project delay reduction. Independent third-party case studies not yet publicly documented as of June 2026.
tools.integrations: Integrates with Procore and other construction platforms per company documentation
tools.spoken: Space AI, from the company of the same name, is a newer AI-powered construction bidding and project management platform founded in 2025. It combines bid tracking, AI competitiveness scoring, scope gap analysis, and award management in one interface. There is a free tier for basic bidding plus a Professional plan at ninety-nine dollars per user per month.

tools.name: XBuild
tools.description: Chat-based agentic AI estimating platform for residential and specialty trade contractors. Users upload measurement reports or describe a project in natural language and receive a complete estimate in approximately 15 minutes. Real-time integration with ABC Supply for current material pricing. Generates Good/Better/Best pricing options with e-sign proposals and Stripe Connect payment links. Launched with residential roofing estimating in January 2026; expanding to concrete, landscaping, painting, windows/doors, glass, insulation, HVAC, and plumbing. Raised $19M Series A (January 2026) led by N47 with Rackhouse Ventures and Andreessen Horowitz.
tools.pricing: Not publicly listed; contact sales
tools.results: 15,000+ projects completed in year one, approximately $250M in construction volume. Real-time ABC Supply material pricing eliminates manual cost lookup.
tools.integrations: ABC Supply (real-time material pricing), EagleView, CompanyCam, Stripe Connect, DocuSign

tools.name: ConstructionBids.ai
tools.description: AI-powered government and public construction bid aggregation platform that consolidates live bid opportunities from 2,000+ federal, state, and local procurement portals into a single AI-scored dashboard. Delivers AI fit scores matched to company trade and geography, automated deadline alerts, scope matching, and AI risk analysis on contract terms. Purpose-built for contractors pursuing government and public construction work.
tools.pricing: Starting at $49/month; AI risk analysis and proposal features at higher tiers (~$99/month+)
tools.results: Consolidates 2,000+ government procurement portals into a single AI-scored feed. $49/month entry price makes government bid sourcing accessible to small specialty subs.
tools.integrations: SAM.gov, state and local government procurement portals

tools.name: Kreo
tools.description: AI-assisted 2D takeoff and 3D BIM cost estimating platform that automatically detects elements, cross-references data across drawing sheets, and generates cost reports. Supports both PDF/2D drawing takeoffs and 3D BIM model (IFC/Revit) cost estimation. Multi-user cloud collaboration allows simultaneous team access. Particularly strong for firms using BIM-based workflows where design revision cost estimates update within minutes rather than days.
tools.pricing: Starting at $35/user/month — one of the most accessible AI estimating platforms currently available
tools.results: Design revision cost updates in minutes vs. days for BIM-heavy projects. $35/user/month entry price makes AI estimating accessible to small sub teams of any size.
tools.integrations: IFC (Revit/BIM format), PDF plan import; cloud-based multi-user access

tools.name: Fresco
tools.description: AI takeoff and estimating platform purpose-built exclusively for Division 8 specialty contractors covering doors, frames, and hardware. Automates the complete takeoff: reads project specifications, cross-references hardware sets against door schedules, builds out complete hardware schedules, and produces a quantity takeoff with 99% claimed accuracy. Reduces Division 8 estimating time by 70%. Y Combinator F24 batch.
tools.pricing: Not publicly listed; contact sales
tools.results: 3,000+ takeoffs completed for GCs and Division 8 specialty subs. 99% claimed accuracy on hardware schedule cross-referencing. 70% reduction in Division 8 estimating time.
tools.integrations: PDF specification import; standard hardware schedule formats

tools.name: Trunk Tools
tools.description: AI-native construction platform providing TrunkText (natural language Q&A across drawings, specs, submittals, RFIs, and contracts via SMS or Microsoft Teams without opening a portal), TrunkSubmittal (automated comparison of subcontractor submittals against specs, surfacing compliance issues), and TrunkReview (drawing revision change detection with narrative summaries). Raised $40M Series B (July 2025) led by Insight Partners with Redpoint Ventures and Innovation Endeavors; total funding $70M. Revenue grew 5x in the prior 12 months. In May 2026 announced Autodesk Forma integration, connecting agents directly to Forma Data Management as a single source of record. Partnership with Microsoft also announced in 2026.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: Revenue grew 5x in the 12 months prior to Series B. Deployed on hundreds of active projects. Field crews can interact via SMS without opening a separate portal.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, Autodesk Forma (May 2026), Microsoft Teams
tools.spoken: Trunk Tools expanded its Autodesk Forma integration in May 2026, adding a full suite of agents including bid analysis, SOP adherence, and scope writing on top of its existing RFI management and submittal review capabilities, all available directly to project teams running Autodesk Forma Build. Its three core products remain TrunkSubmittal for spec-matched submittal review, TrunkText for conversational queries across project documents, and TrunkReview for analyzing changes in newly issued drawing sets. Cleveland Construction reported saving more than seven hundred ninety hours and sixty thousand dollars across four projects. Pricing is by custom quote.

tools.name: Procore AI (Helix / Agent Builder)
tools.description: Procore's embedded AI platform, dramatically expanded through the January 2026 acquisition of Datagrid (vertical AI firm). Includes five prebuilt agentic AI agents: Submittal Review Agent (compares submittals to specs, generates review summaries, flags discrepancies), RFI Agent (checks RFIs for completeness, suggests edits, attaches relevant documents), Deep Search Agent (spans specs, drawings, and RFIs to consolidate references and surface conflicts), Daily Log Agent, and Contract Review Agent. Agents trigger automatically on new submittals, RFIs, or change orders with human approval before actions finalize. On June 1, 2026, Procore relaunched its Common Data Environment with connected data and agentic AI at its core.
tools.pricing: Included in Procore platform; priced on Annual Construction Volume (ACV)
tools.results: Buro Happold on track to reduce construction administration by 50% using Procore AI agents. Q1 2026: Submittals integrated with Scheduling module. Datagrid integration layer is platform-agnostic and also supports Autodesk Construction Cloud, Trimble, and Hilti.
tools.integrations: Native to Procore; Datagrid integration layer also connects to Autodesk Construction Cloud, Trimble, and Hilti
tools.spoken: Procore's AI platform, Procore Helix, continues expanding in 2026 following its Datagrid acquisition in January 2026. On May twenty-first, Procore launched native AI agents including Deep Search, a Daily Log agent, a Submittals agent, an RFI agent, and a Contract Review agent. At CONEXPO and Groundbreak 2026, Procore also unveiled an enhanced Resource view giving general contractors and specialty contractors better workforce planning tools, and a redesigned submittal experience with automated deadline calculations and a new centralized Submittal Plan card that eliminates manual date entry. The embedded AI experience is in private beta with broader availability expected this summer. Procore is priced on annual construction volume.

tools.name: Civils.ai
tools.description: AI platform that extracts data from drawings, specifications, and building codes to enable automated RFI generation, advanced search, and compliance workflow automation.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: Reduces manual review time; catches compliance issues early to reduce RFI back-and-forth cycles.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with major project management platforms
tools.spoken: Civils dot AI extracts data directly from drawings, specs, and building codes to automate RFI generation and compliance tracking. It is built for both GC and specialty sub teams, and pricing is by custom quote.

tools.name: BuildSync
tools.description: Purpose-built AI submittal review platform that compares submitted product data against project specifications with 95%+ accuracy on technical compliance checking. Reduces first-submission rejection rates from 35% to 5% and cuts review time by 70-80%.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: 95%+ accuracy on technical compliance checking; cuts first-submission rejection rates from 35% to 5%; reduces review time 70-80%.
tools.integrations: Procore (automated routing with compliance reports); Autodesk Construction Cloud (two-way sync); API access
tools.spoken: BuildSync is a purpose-built AI submittal review platform that compares submitted product data against project specifications with ninety-five percent accuracy. It cuts first-submission rejection rates from thirty-five percent to five percent and reduces review time by seventy to eighty percent. BuildSync integrates directly with Procore and Autodesk Construction Cloud. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: SubmittalLink
tools.description: AI-powered submittal and RFI management platform purpose-built for subcontractors with transparent volume-based pricing and unlimited users on all plans. Converts spec books into structured submittal logs automatically, manages RFI routing with notification tracking, and uses AI to parse drawings, generate status reports, and automate coversheet creation.
tools.pricing: $150/month (Starter, under $5M annual construction volume); $250/month (Pro, $5M-$25M); Enterprise custom quote above $25M. All plans include unlimited users, projects, and file storage.
tools.results: Unlimited users at no extra cost; AI automatically generates structured submittal logs from spec books; mobile access for field review; automated owner reporting.
tools.integrations: Standalone platform; mobile app for field access
tools.spoken: SubmittalLink is a platform purpose-built for subcontractors managing construction submittals and RFIs on an accessible subscription model. It converts spec books into structured submittal logs automatically, sends notification-driven routing across the team, and uses AI to parse drawings, generate status reports, and automate coversheet creation. All plans include unlimited users, projects, and file storage. Pricing starts at one hundred fifty dollars per month for teams with under five million dollars in annual construction volume, and two hundred fifty dollars per month for five to twenty-five million dollar teams.

tools.name: Constructable
tools.description: AI-native field and project management platform built for mid-market general contractors running $20M-$150M commercial projects. Pins RFIs directly to drawing locations with full context, uses an AI Answer Engine to pull answers from past project data instantly, and provides unlimited users on all plans with no per-seat fees, no ACV audits, and no overage charges. Covers drawings, RFIs, submittals, punch lists, and financial management in one system. Onboards teams in under 21 days.
tools.pricing: $850/month (Field Management); $2,000/month (Field and Project Management); $3,500/month (Field, Project, and Financial Management); unlimited users included
tools.results: Unlimited users at flat rate; onboards teams in under 21 days; RFIs pinned to exact drawing locations; AI Answer Engine surfaces answers instantly from past project data.
tools.integrations: QuickBooks Online, Sage Intacct (financial sync)
tools.spoken: Constructable is an AI-native field and project management platform built for mid-market general contractors running twenty million to one hundred fifty million dollar commercial projects. Constructable pins RFIs directly to the drawing location where questions arise, uses an AI Answer Engine to pull answers from past project data instantly, and includes unlimited users on all plans with no per-seat fees and no overage charges. The platform covers drawings, RFIs, submittals, punch lists, and financial management in one system and onboards teams in under twenty-one days. Pricing starts at eight hundred fifty dollars per month for field management, two thousand dollars per month for field and project management, and three thousand five hundred dollars per month for the complete field, project, and financial management tier.

tools.name: Nomic AI
tools.description: AEC-specialized AI platform built on LLMs trained specifically on construction documentation including drawing sets up to 200 pages and specifications up to 800 pages. Key capabilities include automated submittal review cross-referencing product data against drawings and specifications, building code compliance checking against 380+ building codes, drawing QA/QC analysis, and specification writing assistance. Named to BuiltWorlds 40 AI-Driven AEC Solutions to Watch in 2026.
tools.pricing: $40/user/month with minimum 25-seat commitment; includes $20/seat/month in AI usage credits
tools.results: Named BuiltWorlds 2026 Top 40 AI-Driven AEC Solutions. AEC domain-specific LLM training on 200+ page drawing sets and 800+ page specs distinguishes it from general-purpose AI. 380+ building codes in compliance database.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, Egnyte, SharePoint

tools.name: CompanyCam
tools.description: Field photo documentation and contractor operations platform. Raised $415M Series C (August 2025), becoming the first Nebraska startup to reach a $2B unicorn valuation. Acquired Beam Finance Inc. in March 2026, adding AI estimating, payments, and invoicing to the platform. AI Note and Quick Caption features let field crews talk while taking photos with AI formatting output into structured notes and reports. Strategic direction has evolved from photo documentation into a full contractor operations platform: capture photos, AI-assisted estimate, then get paid.
tools.pricing: From $99/month (3-user minimum); Enterprise custom pricing
tools.results: $2B valuation as of August 2025. 200,000+ contractor users. AI features in Premium and Elite tiers.
tools.integrations: Procore, Buildertrend, JobNimbus, QuickBooks, Xero, and 20+ other platforms
tools.spoken: CompanyCam is a photo-first field documentation platform that achieved unicorn status after closing a Series C funding round in August 2025. Its AI Walkthrough Note feature lets a superintendent walk the site, speak observations, and snap photos, and AI automatically assembles a structured daily report. CompanyCam acquired Beam Finance in March 2026 to add AI estimating, payments, and invoicing to the platform. Plans start at ninety-nine dollars per month with a three-user minimum.

tools.name: eSUB
tools.description: Mobile-first field management platform built exclusively for commercial specialty subcontractors. Added meaningful AI capabilities in 2026 including automated daily report drafting from field supervisor voice notes, predictive labor cost analysis flagging tasks trending over budget, and intelligent RFI drafting that suggests relevant specification sections.
tools.pricing: Custom quote; positioned as more affordable than Procore for sub-only teams
tools.results: AI daily report drafting reduced foreman paperwork from 45 minutes to 10 minutes per day. 95% of customers fully operational within 90 days.
tools.integrations: Sage 300 (integration released), major accounting platforms, drawing and design platforms
tools.spoken: eSUB is the subcontractor-exclusive field management platform that has added meaningful AI capabilities in 2026, including automated daily report drafting from field supervisor voice notes, predictive labor cost analysis that flags tasks trending over budget, and intelligent RFI drafting that suggests relevant specification sections. A mechanical sub using the AI daily report drafting feature reduced foreman paperwork from forty-five minutes to ten minutes per day. Pricing is by custom quote.

tools.name: Buildots
tools.description: AI construction progress tracking and workforce intelligence platform using 360-degree cameras worn on hardhats to automatically compare actual progress against the project schedule. Raised $45M Series D (May 2025) led by Qumra Capital; total raised $166M. Acquired Genda (workforce and safety management platform trusted by DPR, Hensel Phelps, Clark Construction, and JE Dunn) in October 2025, combining visual progress data with labor and equipment insights. In April 2026 launched under the Construction Intelligence positioning, unifying fragmented site data into a living operational model. On track to quadruple North American presence in 2025.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on project size
tools.results: Projects report up to 50% reduction in delays (2-3 months saved). Customers include DPR, Hensel Phelps, Clark Construction, and JE Dunn (via Genda).
tools.integrations: Oracle Primavera P6, Asta Power Project, Microsoft Project, Procore
tools.spoken: Buildots has added major new construction firms to its customer roster in 2026, now including STO Building Group, Mortenson, Ledcor, and Pomerleau alongside existing clients Turner Construction, JE Dunn, Digital Realty, and Intel. The platform uses helmet-mounted cameras and computer vision to capture real-time site progress against the schedule. Total funding reached one hundred sixty-six million dollars on a forty-five million dollar Series D. Pricing is custom-quoted by project size.

tools.name: Raken
tools.description: All-in-one field management platform for construction subcontractors and GCs covering daily reporting, time tracking, scheduling, safety management, workforce management, and project management. Sverica Capital Management (a growth-oriented PE firm specializing in technology) acquired a majority stake in September 2025; management team retained. New AI features include AI Photo ID for biometric clock-in (facial identity verification) and frictionless RFI creation as a new project management module. Added QuickBooks Desktop and CMiC integrations in 2025. 4,500+ construction firms and 70,000+ users.
tools.pricing: $15/user/month (Lite), $25/user/month (Pro); annual billing saves ~15%; 15-day free trial
tools.results: 4,500+ construction firms and 70,000+ users. Sverica Capital investment in September 2025 signals significant growth push.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, QuickBooks, major payroll platforms
tools.spoken: Raken received a majority stake investment from Sverica Capital in September 2025, giving the field reporting platform new private equity backing to accelerate product development and market expansion. Raken now serves more than forty-five hundred construction firms and seventy thousand users with daily reports, time tracking, toolbox talks, and production tracking. Pricing starts at fifteen dollars per user per month for the Lite plan and twenty-five dollars per user per month for Pro.

tools.name: BuildOps
tools.description: Unified commercial contractor management platform for specialty trades covering both service operations and construction project management. Raised $127M Series C (March 2025) achieving unicorn status at $1B valuation; total funding $225.8M. Revenue reached $97.4M in 2025, up from $51.9M in 2024. In May 2026 launched BuildOps Projects: an AI-native construction project management system for specialty trades covering multi-month work from kickoff to closeout, including RFIs, submittals, change orders, retainage tracking, and subcontract management. OpsAI (embedded AI model) surfaces margin slips in real time, auto-generates invoices from technician field notes, and converts POs to invoice line items. Leadership hired CMO from Autodesk and CPO from Procore in 2025-2026.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: 1,500+ specialty contractors on platform. Revenue grew 88% year-over-year in 2025. BuildOps Projects delivers the first AI-native system built specifically for specialty trades to run multi-month construction work.
tools.integrations: QuickBooks, Sage, ServiceTitan workflow compatibility; Projects module integrates billing, change orders, and field data in one system
tools.spoken: BuildOps launched its Projects module in May 2026, giving specialty contractors an AI-native system to run multi-month construction work from kickoff to closeout alongside their service operations. Powered by OpsAI for scheduling, document management, field coordination, and daily reporting. BuildOps raised one hundred twenty-seven million dollars in a Series C round in 2026, reaching a one billion dollar valuation. Pricing is by custom quote.

tools.name: InspectMind AI
tools.description: Y Combinator-backed AI platform for construction field inspections and plan checking. Field Reports product captures site photos and multi-language voice notes on mobile, works offline, and generates structured daily reports, punch lists, and site walkthroughs using AI.
tools.pricing: $100/user/month (Field Reports); plan checking from $100/check or ~$1/drawing sheet at scale
tools.results: Generates field reports in minutes vs. hours; multi-language support including Spanish and Chinese to English; offline functionality for remote sites.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud
tools.spoken: InspectMind AI is a Y Combinator-backed platform that generates field reports, punch lists, safety inspections, and site walkthroughs in minutes. Its Field Reports product captures photos and voice notes on mobile, works offline, and produces structured reports at one hundred dollars per user per month. Plan checking starts at one hundred dollars per review. It integrates with Procore and Autodesk Construction Cloud.

tools.name: iFieldSmart
tools.description: Construction management platform with reality capture and AI-powered field documentation. Its Lens360 feature converts a single 360-degree site walkthrough into a complete digital twin with actionable insights, documentation, and progress tracking. Includes RFI and submittal tracking, task scheduling, and risk management.
tools.pricing: Starting at $14.99/user/month; free trial available; custom pricing for enterprise
tools.results: Single 360 walkthrough generates full site documentation and digital twin intelligence; reduces site visit time and documentation overhead.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, Airtasks, Assemble
tools.spoken: iFieldSmart from iFieldSmart Technologies is a construction management platform with reality capture and AI-powered field documentation. Its Lens three-sixty feature turns a single site walkthrough into a complete digital twin. Pricing starts at fourteen dollars and ninety-nine cents per user per month.

tools.name: Kahua
tools.description: Enterprise construction project management platform for large GCs, program managers, and public sector owners. In May 2026 launched Noa, an embedded AI assistant that automates workflows, improves cost and reporting visibility, and manages capital programs through natural language interaction. Kahua Enterprise AI is a FedRAMP-authorized suite for secure automated data collection and project workflow management. Added IBM Maximo integration (November 2025) bridging construction project delivery with asset lifecycle management. US Army Corps of Engineers and Infrastructure Ontario are active Kahua customers.
tools.pricing: Custom quote; implementation costs typically $5,000-$50,000+ depending on scale
tools.results: Named Top Construction Technology Firm in Construction Executive's 2025 Top Tech report. US Army Corps of Engineers and Infrastructure Ontario deployments. UK Ministry of Defence cybersecurity accreditation achieved.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with major accounting and project management platforms
tools.spoken: Kahua is a flexible construction management platform with built-in AI through its Noa assistant, which automates repetitive project workflows, uncovers insights from project data, and lets teams build custom apps in minutes. Pricing is custom-quoted with implementation costs typically ranging from five thousand to fifty thousand dollars or more.

tools.name: OpenSpace
tools.description: AI-powered 360-degree site capture and visual intelligence platform. Acquired Disperse (AI construction progress tracking company) in October 2025, following a June 2025 partnership. In February 2026 launched OpenSpace Field in general availability: an image-based, field-first task and issue management system that extends OpenSpace from a system of visual record into a system of visual action. AI Autolocation automatically tags where an issue occurred on site without manual pinning. AI Voice Notes enable hands-free capture that auto-fills form fields from speech. Total funding $199M across 7 rounds.
tools.pricing: Annual Construction Volume (ACV)-based; Core and Enterprise tiers; custom quote
tools.results: Disperse acquisition adds AI progress tracking for structural and MEP milestones. OpenSpace Field GA February 2026. Bidirectional integrations used by ENR Top 400 GCs.
tools.integrations: Procore (bidirectional), Autodesk Construction Cloud (bidirectional)
tools.spoken: OpenSpace is a Visual Intelligence Platform that uses three-sixty cameras to automatically map site photos to floor plans and BIM models. It launched OpenSpace Field in general availability in February 2026, an image-based field task and issue management solution that connects captured site reality directly to execution workflows. Suffolk Construction documented an eighty-six percent speed improvement in documenting issues using the platform's AI Autolocation and AI Voice Notes. Vinci Construction UK saved six thousand or more hours annually on documentation. Pricing is based on annual construction volume.

tools.name: Novade
tools.description: AI-powered construction field management platform used by 100,000+ users in 25+ countries. Key AI features include speech-to-forms, scan-to-form, AI-powered daily report generation, and a predictive safety monitoring engine with up to 88% accuracy for hazard risk scoring. Platform covers quality checklists, safety inspections, daily reports, punch lists, and project management workflows. $21.3M total funding.
tools.pricing: Free plan (up to 5 users, 5 projects); Standard and Premium plans with custom pricing; Enterprise plan for large deployments
tools.results: 100,000+ users across 25+ countries. AI predictive safety monitoring achieves up to 88% accuracy. Speech-to-forms and scan-to-form eliminate manual field data entry. $21.3M total funding.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, Oracle; IoT system API connections
tools.spoken: Novade Solutions is an AI-powered field management platform used by more than one hundred thousand users in twenty-five or more countries. Key AI features include speech-to-forms that convert field voice observations into structured mobile data, scan-to-form that photographs paper forms and converts them into digital templates, AI daily report generation, and a predictive safety monitoring engine trained on real-world incident data that achieves up to eighty-eight percent accuracy in hazard risk scoring. A free plan is available for up to five users and five projects.

tools.name: Benetics AI
tools.description: Voice-activated AI platform for construction field documentation including daily reporting, punch lists, and RFI creation, with real-time translation across 30+ languages. Expanded into US and Canada markets in 2025, with active deployments in Michigan. December 2025: expanded REST API with Make.com and Zapier connectors added. January 2026: launched automatic detection of construction plans arriving in email. Email Assistant beta planned for Q3 2026. Now serves 250+ construction firms across Europe and North America. Showcased at Procore Groundbreak 2025.
tools.pricing: Tiered per-user plans (Solo, Team, Enterprise); free trial available; custom Enterprise pricing for 200+ employee firms
tools.results: 250+ construction firms across Europe and North America. Expanding in US/Canada market with Michigan deployments.
tools.integrations: Standalone mobile app (iOS and Android); voice-to-structured report workflow
tools.spoken: Benetics AI is an AI voice assistant for construction field documentation. Field crews speak naturally into the mobile app to capture site conditions, deliveries, safety observations, and progress updates, and AI converts voice to professional structured reports in seconds without stopping work. Two hundred twenty or more companies are using the platform across the US and Canada with users reporting up to seventy to eighty percent reduction in admin time. Available on iOS and Android.

tools.name: BuilderHelp
tools.description: AI-driven construction management platform targeting $5M-$65M general contractors, launched June 1, 2026. Features predictive AI scheduling with proactive timeline adjustments before delays occur, centralized communication across subcontractors, owners, and project teams, and enterprise-grade project management in a simplified interface. Developed by construction industry professionals rather than traditional software engineers. Based in Auburn, Alabama. Over 50 GCs on the waitlist at launch.
tools.pricing: Not publicly listed; contact company directly
tools.results: Launched June 1, 2026 with 50+ GC waitlist. Built by industry professionals targeting real-world jobsite communication and administrative challenges for $5M-$65M general contractors.
tools.integrations: Not yet published; platform launched June 2026
tools.spoken: BuilderHelp from BuilderHelp launched its AI construction management platform on June first, twenty twenty-six, targeting general contractors in the five million to sixty-five million dollar range. The platform was developed by industry professionals and features predictive AI scheduling, centralized communication across subs and project teams, task tracking with real-time progress visibility, invoice and budget monitoring, and document management for plans, specs, permits, and contracts. More than fifty general contractors were on the waitlist at launch. Pricing is available directly through the company.

tools.name: KYRO AI
tools.description: Unified field management platform for construction, vegetation management, and storm restoration contractors. Combines project management, crew communication, time tracking, payroll, and daily reporting in one mobile app. AI automatically translates voice notes from field crews into any language. OCR scans documents and auto-generates schedules from uploaded files. Built for small-to-mid-size specialty contractors including those in utility, vegetation management, and restoration work. Available on iOS, Android, and web.
tools.pricing: $21/user/month (Basic, billed annually); $42/user/month (Pro, billed annually). Free trial available.
tools.built_for: Specialty subcontractors and small GCs, particularly utility, vegetation management, and storm restoration contractors
tools.results: No independently documented case studies published yet. Platform commercially available.
tools.integrations: Standalone platform; no confirmed Procore or Autodesk integrations.

tools.name: Opusense AI
tools.description: AI-powered inspection and field reporting tool for construction, engineering, and property teams. Field inspectors speak observations aloud and take photos; AI generates a structured, branded inspection report in minutes. Claims up to 80% reduction in report production time. AI learns company writing style to maintain consistent voice across all reports. Y Combinator-backed startup (2025). Integrates with Fieldwire, converting observations into Fieldwire tasks in one click.
tools.pricing: Not publicly listed; contact for pricing.
tools.built_for: Field inspectors, construction engineers, and contractors requiring formal inspection documentation
tools.results: Up to 80% reduction in report production time (company-reported). No independent case studies yet.
tools.integrations: Fieldwire (observations convert to tasks in one click)

tools.name: Klutch AI
tools.description: Multi-agent AI construction management platform offering specialized agents across the project lifecycle: Archie AI for zoning and permit review (up to 50% faster permit cycles), Bob AI for capturing punch items, safety forms, and daily jobsite updates via SMS, WhatsApp, and Telegram without requiring a portal login, Petra AI for takeoffs, estimates, bid leveling, and vendor scoring, and Hailey AI for warranty ticket management linked to vendor performance. Raised $8M seed round (June 2025) led by Bain Capital Ventures and Bling Capital with Brick and Mortar Ventures and angels from Autodesk and BuildZoom.
tools.pricing: Not publicly listed; contact sales
tools.results: Saves 10+ hours/week per project manager. Petra AI boosts PM capacity by 35%. Covers pre-construction through project closeout in a single agent-driven platform.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud (planned)

tools.name: Opusense AI
tools.description: Y Combinator-backed (X25 batch) AI-powered field inspection report automation. Field inspectors walk a site, speak observations aloud, and snap photos; AI generates a detailed, properly formatted inspection report in real time without interrupting fieldwork. Designed for construction special inspectors, civil engineers, field quality control personnel, and infrastructure consultants. Available on iOS and Android with offline capability.
tools.pricing: Not publicly listed; contact sales
tools.results: Claims up to 80% reduction in inspection report production time. Y Combinator X25 batch backing. Available on iOS and Android with offline mode for remote sites.
tools.integrations: iOS and Android mobile; cloud report storage and sharing

tools.name: Hardline
tools.description: Voice-first construction documentation tool that captures jobsite phone calls and verbal conversations in English and Spanish, transcribes them in real time, and uses AI to extract and structure action items into daily logs, RFIs, punch lists, change orders, tasks, and meeting notes automatically from the phone call itself. Targets the reality that the phone call is the primary communication medium on most job sites. Suffolk Technologies BOOST Accelerator alumni. Raised $2M pre-seed led by Mucker Capital. Active across 10 states and 2 countries.
tools.pricing: Flexible early-access pricing by team size; contact sales
tools.results: Saves superintendents up to 3 hours/day in documentation time. Active across 10 states and 2 countries. Captures structured documentation from phone calls with no extra steps required from field staff.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Forma, Hilti Fieldwire

tools.name: Track3D
tools.description: AI-powered Reality Intelligence platform that unifies 360 cameras, drones, laser scanners, and LiDAR data into a single platform (VisualTrack) and applies AI-driven construction progress tracking (ProgressTrack). Quantifies work installed by trade and activity, generates automated progress reports, and enables payment milestone verification from captured site data. Differentiates from single-source platforms by ingesting data from multiple capture hardware types simultaneously. Named to BuiltWorlds 40 AI-Driven AEC Solutions to Watch in 2026.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on project scale and capture hardware
tools.results: Named BuiltWorlds 2026 Top 40 AI-Driven AEC Solutions. Unifies 360, drone, laser scan, and LiDAR data for contractors running complex projects with mixed documentation equipment.
tools.integrations: Autodesk Construction Cloud, Procore

tools.name: HCSS HeavyJob
tools.description: AI-enhanced field operations and cost management platform for heavy civil and specialty contractors. 2026 Winter Release added HCSS Copilot AI: summarizes the Daily Digest, helps users navigate the platform, and answers questions using company knowledge base and job data. 2026 Spring Release added an AI-powered Summarize tool for audit logs (condenses massive audit trail logs into actionable summaries in one click), direct HeavyBid-to-HeavyJob estimate handoff, and Docs and Plans integrated directly into the HeavyJob mobile app. HCSS Field app renamed HeavyJob (platform consolidation). CONEXPO 2026 featured HeavyJob Map View and Resource Planner.
tools.pricing: $60-$4,000+/user/year depending on modules; custom enterprise pricing
tools.results: Used by thousands of heavy civil contractors across North America. HCSS Copilot AI and Spring 2026 AI audit summaries reduce manual data review time significantly.
tools.integrations: Major construction accounting systems
tools.spoken: HCSS HeavyJob is the go-to standard for heavy and civil specialty contractors doing time-and-material work. It pulls T and M billing data directly from field time cards and generates accurate invoices with minimal manual effort. Pricing ranges from sixty to over four thousand dollars per user per year depending on modules selected.

tools.name: FOUNDATION T&M Billing Module
tools.description: T&M billing and construction accounting platform backed by Thoma Bravo private equity. Acquired Vendrix, Inc. in June 2025, adding corporate cards, expense management, AP automation, and bill pay to cover nearly every stage of a construction project's financial lifecycle. Core features include time-and-material billing, certified payroll, subcontract management, and job costing. SubBase continues as a procurement integration partner.
tools.pricing: Custom quote as part of FOUNDATION accounting suite
tools.results: Produces precise T&M invoices; deep integration with construction accounting eliminates duplicate data entry.
tools.integrations: Sage, QuickBooks, Procore (via SubBase partner integration), Autodesk, Viewpoint; Vendrix adds AP automation and corporate card management
tools.spoken: FOUNDATION Software's T and M Billing Module captures every billable expense, automates markups, and produces client-ready invoices for time-and-material contracts. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: SmartBarrel
tools.description: AI biometric workforce management system combining facial recognition time tracking with PPE compliance monitoring at clock-in, validating each worker against a saved facial profile even in jobsite conditions.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on site count, worker count, and hardware deployment
tools.results: Eliminates buddy-punching; provides accurate T&M labor documentation; adds PPE compliance monitoring layer to time tracking hardware.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with payroll and project management systems
tools.spoken: SmartBarrel combines AI biometric facial recognition for time tracking with PPE compliance monitoring at clock-in, giving specialty subs a dual-purpose tool. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: Rhumbix
tools.description: Mobile-first workforce tracking and T&M billing platform for construction. Autodesk completed its acquisition of Rhumbix on March 31, 2026, bringing real-time jobsite labor and payroll data into its construction platform. Rhumbix continues to operate products and customer support without changes; integration into Autodesk Construction Cloud and Autodesk Forma is underway.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on user count
tools.results: 50% reduction in T&M processing time; digital T&M tag processing reduced from 2-6 weeks to 1-2 days; cost overruns caught 2-3 weeks earlier. Acquisition closed March 31, 2026. Integration into Autodesk Construction Cloud and Autodesk Forma underway.
tools.integrations: Autodesk Construction Cloud (acquisition integration in progress); Procore; QuickBooks Online Advanced; ADP Workforce Now
tools.spoken: Rhumbix, now acquired by Autodesk with the deal closing March thirty-first, twenty twenty-six, was a mobile-first time and materials tracking platform that automates T and M ticket generation directly from field time cards and captures unplanned work in real time. Integration into Autodesk Construction Cloud and Autodesk Forma is underway, with no changes to products or customer support in the near term. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: Siteline
tools.description: Billing and payment management platform built exclusively for commercial subcontractors. Automates pay applications in any custom GC format, manages lien waivers with AI validation (automatically flags missing signatures, incorrect amounts, and mismatched details), and handles accounts receivable reporting, collections management, and cash flow forecasting. AI lien waiver validation is in active production for all customers.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on billing volume and integrations
tools.results: Customers save an average of 7 days per week across billing workflows. Payments sped up by an average of 3 weeks. AI lien waiver validation in production.
tools.integrations: Viewpoint Vista (two-way real-time sync), Procore Pay, Textura, GCPay, Foundation Software
tools.spoken: Siteline is the billing and pay application platform built exclusively for commercial specialty subcontractors. It automates AIA pay app preparation, lien waiver collection, and vendor compliance tracking. Customers bill six times faster and reduce invoice aging by thirty percent on average. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: Adaptive
tools.description: AI-native construction accounting platform that continuously captures and codes every cost (bills, receipts, cards, payments) to the correct job and cost code in real time. Builds accurate cost-plus and fixed-price billings directly from approved job costs with backup documentation attached. Automates job costing, WIP reporting, billing approvals, payments, and financial reporting. Won Best of IBS 2026 Award in the Business Solution Software category at the NAHB International Builders' Show (February 2026). Raised $19M Series A (July 2024) led by Emergence Capital with Andreessen Horowitz participating; total raised $26M.
tools.pricing: Starts at $575/month for businesses under $5M in annual revenue; revenue-based tiers above that threshold
tools.results: Best of IBS 2026 Award winner (Business Solution Software category). No project-level case studies published yet.
tools.integrations: QuickBooks (real-time two-way sync); API integrations with construction management platforms
tools.spoken: Adaptive is an AI-native construction accounting platform that deploys AI agents to automate manual accounting work and deliver real-time financial control. Pricing starts at five hundred seventy-five dollars per month for businesses with up to five million dollars in annual revenue.

tools.name: SubBase
tools.description: AI-powered materials procurement and invoice reconciliation platform for specialty subcontractors and self-performing GCs. Uses AI to automatically compare purchase orders, invoices, and delivery tickets for three-way reconciliation, completing the process in an average of 21 seconds vs. 3-5 minutes manually. AI reconciliation feature has processed 4,000+ invoices covering $3.5M+ in purchases since launch.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: 21-second average AI reconciliation vs. 3-5 minutes manual. 4,000+ invoices processed since feature launch, covering $3.5M+ in purchases. Automates invoice approval to a single click after AI validation.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with accounting and ERP systems
tools.spoken: SubBase is an AI-powered materials procurement platform for specialty subcontractors that uses AI to automatically compare purchase orders, invoices, and delivery tickets for three-way reconciliation, completing the process in an average of twenty-one seconds compared to three to five minutes of manual work. Since the feature launched, SubBase has processed more than four thousand invoices covering over three and a half million dollars in purchases. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: Payra
tools.description: AI-enabled accounts receivable automation platform that embeds into existing construction ERPs to enable ACH and card digital payment acceptance, automate cash application, and reconcile payments to invoices in real time. Raised $15M growth investment from Edison Partners (February 2026) — first outside capital after being previously bootstrapped. Funding targets deeper ERP integration partnerships and go-to-market expansion across the construction sector.
tools.pricing: Not publicly listed; pricing structured around ERP integration and transaction volume.
tools.results: 20% reduction in Days Sales Outstanding, 75% decrease in past-due invoices, significant admin time savings (customer-reported). $15M Edison Partners investment validates commercial traction.
tools.integrations: Trimble Viewpoint, Foundation Software, Sage, NetSuite, and other construction ERPs
tools.spoken: Payra is a B2B accounts receivable automation platform built for construction contractors running on legacy ERP systems, raising fifteen million dollars from Edison Partners in February 2026. It integrates directly with Trimble Viewpoint, FOUNDATION, Sage, and others to automate cash application and payment reconciliation without replacing existing systems. Customers have documented a twenty percent reduction in days sales outstanding and a seventy-five percent drop in past-due invoices. Pricing is transaction-based.

tools.name: Intuit Enterprise Suite Construction Edition
tools.description: AI-native ERP built specifically for mid-market construction companies, launched February 11, 2026 and available in open beta at no additional cost to existing Intuit Enterprise Suite customers. Features project phases for cash flow and profitability tracking, cost groups by category (labor, materials, equipment, subcontractor), AIA-style invoicing at the phase level, negative change orders, and an AI Project Management Agent with real-time cost recommendations. Spring 2026 expansion added automated multi-entity close workflows, dimensional reporting with peer benchmarking, integrated Human Capital Management, and enhanced AI agents including a Finance Agent and Accounting Agent with batch bank feed processing.
tools.pricing: Starting approximately $1,400/month. Spring 2026 rollout expanded to all US IES customers; no longer limited to open beta. Typical $25M contractor pays $1,400-$2,000/month.
tools.results: First AI-powered construction-specific ERP from Intuit. AIA-style invoicing tracks contract value, invoiced to date, and remaining balance at phase level. Open beta at no extra cost since February 2026.
tools.integrations: QuickBooks Online Advanced; Intuit Enterprise Suite ecosystem
tools.spoken: Intuit launched its Enterprise Suite Construction Edition on February eleventh, twenty twenty-six, delivering a purpose-built AI-powered ERP for mid-market contractors. It includes project phases with AI-enabled cost group tracking, AIA-style invoicing, negative change orders, proposal tools with e-signatures, and an AI Project Management Agent for cash flow and profitability monitoring. It is currently in open beta at no additional cost for existing Intuit Enterprise Suite customers, and available as a paid add-on for QuickBooks Online Advanced.

tools.name: Trimble Pay
tools.description: Electronic management of payment applications, lien waivers, and subcontractor compliance documentation with integrated ACH payments, operating on a rapid monthly release cadence in 2026. Version 5.3 (March), 5.4 (March), 5.5 (April), 5.7 (May 2026) each added capabilities including ACH payment return email notifications for both GCs and subcontractors and manual mark-as-paid capability for Vista-integrated payments when automated ERP callbacks fail. Fully integrated into the Trimble Construction One ecosystem.
tools.pricing: Free for subcontractors to submit billing documents; GC platform pricing by custom quote as part of Trimble Construction One
tools.results: Journey Group documented a significant reduction in payment processing time using Trimble Pay automation. Eliminates risks from paper checks and delays from missing lien waivers. Native integration with Trimble Spectrum and Vista eliminates duplicate data entry.
tools.integrations: Trimble Viewpoint Vista (deep integration), Trimble Construction One ecosystem
tools.spoken: Trimble Pay, formerly Flashtract and fully integrated into Trimble Construction One following Trimble's 2024 acquisition, streamlines pay application submission, lien waiver collection and storage, certificate of insurance tracking, and compliance document management for both GCs and subcontractors. It is free for subcontractors to submit billing documents when invited by a primary contractor.

tools.name: inBuild
tools.description: AI-powered AP invoice processing platform for construction companies that bridges Procore and QuickBooks. Syncs invoices and commitments from Outlook and Procore, uses AI to automatically match incoming invoices against commitments, assign cost codes, and sync approved invoices to QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Desktop (QBD integration launched January 2026). Provides real-time analytics on invoice processing efficiency. Fills a critical workflow gap for contractors using Procore for project management and QuickBooks for accounting.
tools.pricing: Not publicly listed; listed on Capterra for reference.
tools.built_for: Subcontractors and GCs using both Procore and QuickBooks
tools.results: No specific documented case studies published yet. Commercially available and listed on Capterra.
tools.integrations: Procore (native), QuickBooks Online, QuickBooks Desktop

tools.name: BlueCollar Projects
tools.description: AI-powered construction ERP built natively on NetSuite, launched February 17, 2026. Provides job costing, project tracking, revenue recognition, multi-entity operations, change order management, and subcontractor compliance and lien waiver tracking from a single platform. Time entries from employees and subcontractors feed directly into job costing and payroll. Designed for specialty trades, general contractors, and service-based construction companies that need enterprise-grade financials with construction-specific workflows.
tools.pricing: NetSuite-based pricing — most contractors pay $2,000–$8,000/month depending on modules selected and user count; per-module implementation averages ~$8,000
tools.results: Launched February 2026; early adopter results not yet publicly documented. Platform built on NetSuite's enterprise ERP foundation with construction-specific AI workflows.
tools.integrations: Native NetSuite integration; job costing feeds to NetSuite GL and payroll
tools.spoken: BlueCollar Projects, from BlueCollar Cloud Solutions, is a new AI-powered construction ERP launched in February 2026 and built natively on NetSuite. It handles job costing, project financials, change orders, revenue recognition, and subcontractor lien waiver tracking all in one platform. Pricing typically runs two thousand to eight thousand dollars per month depending on the number of modules and users.

tools.name: Trayd
tools.description: AI-powered back office operating system built exclusively for specialty trade contractors. Automates payroll (union rules, multi-state tax, prevailing wage, certified payroll), HR administration, scheduling with geolocation-based clocking, real-time job costing (labor, equipment, materials), and compliance reporting. Reduces 14 hours of weekly manual payroll work to under 30 minutes through AI automation. Claims 31x productivity gains. Raised $10M Series A (March 2026) led by White Star Capital in 3 weeks, with Y Combinator (repeat), Suffolk Technologies, and RXR Realty. Total funding $17M.
tools.pricing: Not publicly listed; contact sales
tools.results: 600% YoY revenue growth. Reduces 14 hours of weekly payroll work to under 30 minutes (31x productivity gain). Moves tens of millions in payroll weekly across hundreds of specialty trade contractors.
tools.integrations: Payroll and compliance platforms; construction ERP integrations

tools.name: BlueCollar
tools.description: AI-powered construction ERP built natively on NetSuite, combining NetSuite's enterprise financial foundation with construction-specific workflows including job costing, project phase tracking, AIA-style billing, retentions, subcontracts, change order management, revenue recognition, and multi-entity operations. AI layer analyzes financial and operational data in real time to identify cost trends, forecast project outcomes, and flag margin risks. Launched February 2026 targeting mid-market contractors already on or migrating to NetSuite.
tools.pricing: NetSuite-based subscription; contact sales
tools.results: Eliminates the gap between construction project delivery and enterprise financial reporting for contractors in the NetSuite ecosystem without requiring separate construction accounting software.
tools.integrations: NetSuite (native), major construction management platforms via API

tools.name: Vergo
tools.description: AI-powered accounts payable automation and expense management platform built for construction. AI extracts line-item data from invoices and receipts, detects duplicate submissions, routes approvals to correct project managers with job number and cost code suggestions, and syncs with construction ERPs without manual re-entry. Field workers capture receipts on mobile and assign job numbers, cost codes, and cost types from the field. Streamlines T&M cost capture from initial field documentation through AP approval and ERP posting.
tools.pricing: Not publicly listed; contact sales
tools.results: Eliminates manual invoice re-entry and duplicate payment risk. Accelerates AP workflows for specialty subs managing high invoice volumes across multiple active projects.
tools.integrations: QuickBooks, Buildertrend, construction ERP systems

tools.name: Pillar Construction OS
tools.description: AI operating system for construction companies that automates procurement, invoicing, workforce management, financial reporting, and back-office operations in a single platform. AI pulls data from accounting systems, bank feeds, site reports, and WhatsApp communications to automate estimates, invoicing, site accounting, and margin tracking. Raised EUR 12M seed round (May 2026) led by Earlybird Venture Capital and Base10 Partners; total funding EUR 15.2M. Founded in Italy with 400+ active contractor customers; planning European and Latin American expansion. Note: This is a different company from Pillar Technologies, which makes construction site environmental sensors.
tools.pricing: Not publicly listed; contact sales
tools.results: 400+ contractor customers, 2,000+ projects, EUR 1M ARR achieved in first 4 months of 2026. 460,000+ invoices processed. Customers save an average of 14 hours/week on administrative work.
tools.integrations: Accounting systems, bank feeds, WhatsApp communications integration

tools.name: Document Crunch
tools.company: Document Crunch (acquisition by Trimble announced April 2, 2026; pending close as of June 4, 2026)
tools.description: AI contract analysis and risk management platform for construction, deployed on 400+ customers processing $350B+ in construction volume across 10,000+ project kickoffs since 2019. Trimble announced a definitive agreement to acquire Document Crunch on April 2, 2026, with the transaction expected to close Q2 or Q3 2026; as of June 4, 2026, the close has not been publicly confirmed. Upon close, Document Crunch will integrate into Trimble Construction One as an intelligent DNA layer, automatically pushing contract obligations and compliance requirements into Trimble project delivery workflows. ENR reported plans for agentic AI contract review capabilities post-integration. The acquisition will be reported under Trimble's AECO segment.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: 400+ customers, $350B+ in construction volume processed, 10,000+ project kickoffs. Trimble acquisition values the company as a core strategic asset for the Trimble Construction One ecosystem.
tools.integrations: Trimble Construction One (integration planned post-acquisition close); prior Trimble ProjectSight integration
tools.spoken: Document Crunch is the AI contract review platform that reads construction contracts, specs, and insurance documents and surfaces risk clauses, change order triggers, and compliance obligations in plain language. Trimble announced its acquisition of Document Crunch on April 2, 2026, with the deal expected to close in the second quarter of 2026 and full integration planned into the Trimble Construction One ecosystem. The platform now serves more than four hundred customers processing three hundred fifty billion dollars in construction volume across ten thousand project kickoffs.

tools.name: Procore Change Orders with AI Assist
tools.description: Procore's native change order management with AI assistance, significantly expanded through the January 2026 acquisition of Datagrid. New agentic AI includes a Contract Review Agent that analyzes change order language, a Deep Search Agent spanning specs and drawings, and agents for submittals, RFIs, and daily logs. Modernized Change Orders UI (early 2026) delivers faster list view and improved search for large CO volumes. On June 1, 2026, Procore announced a redefined Common Data Environment with connected data and agentic AI, placing change orders in a fully connected project intelligence layer.
tools.pricing: Included in Procore ACV-based subscription
tools.results: Buro Happold on track to reduce construction administration by 50% using Procore AI agents. Change orders approved 8 days faster on average (Procore 2022 ROI study, still widely cited). Datagrid integration layer also supports Autodesk, Trimble, and Hilti users.
tools.integrations: Native to Procore; integrates with Autodesk, Sage, Viewpoint, and 400+ tools
tools.spoken: Procore's change order management with AI Assist lets project teams identify scope gaps, draft change order language, and track financial impacts in real time. Pricing is on annual construction volume.

tools.name: Pelles AI
tools.description: AI document intelligence platform built specifically for trade contractors (MEP, mechanical, electrical, plumbing). Automatically reads full spec sets and generates submittal logs with exact spec citations. DoubleCheck surfaces every scope requirement before it becomes a costly miss. GenAI generates proposals, RFIs, and submittal logs in user-defined formats. Compare module flags drawing and addenda deltas as they drop. Processes project email (Outlook) for RFIs, submittals, and approvals. Added Autodesk Construction Cloud integration in January 2026 connecting to Autodesk Build, Autodesk Docs, and BuildingConnected.
tools.pricing: Custom quote
tools.results: Handles more document volume with greater confidence; enables small teams to compete on documentation quality with larger firms.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud including Autodesk Build and Autodesk Docs (January 2026), BuildingConnected, SharePoint, Bluebeam, Smartsheet
tools.spoken: Pelles AI is a generative AI platform that creates RFIs, submittal logs, and proposals in your company's existing format, giving smaller teams the output speed of a much larger document production operation. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: Clearstory
tools.description: AI-powered change order intelligence platform for GCs and large specialty contractors. In March 2026, Suffolk Construction named Clearstory the standard for change order management across its national portfolio, joining Turner Construction which has deployed the platform on 350+ projects. New 2026 features include AI Reason Categories (auto-classifies change orders by root cause), Bulk AI Import (brings entire COR backlogs into the system), Sage Intacct integration, and Procore Contract Mapping (auto-populates contract obligations). In May 2026, launched the COR Pricing Agent (entered closed beta May 28, 2026): contractors describe a scope change in plain language, AI drafts the change order request and suggests pricing, asking follow-up questions when context is missing. $3.5B in change orders flow through the platform monthly; 1.3M total change orders processed. Specialty contractors report 75% faster approvals and 70%+ processing time reduction.
tools.pricing: Basic tier free for all stakeholders; Standard and Professional paid tiers; implementation $1,000-$5,000
tools.results: Deployed on 350+ projects (Turner). Specialty contractors achieve 65% productivity improvement, saving 7-10 hours/week per person in administrative time. $3.5B in change orders processed monthly; 1.3M total processed. Suffolk Construction adopted as national standard in March 2026.
tools.integrations: Procore (native, with new Contract Mapping March 2026), Sage Intacct Construction (new March 2026), Viewpoint
tools.spoken: Clearstory has had a major 2026 with significant new deployments and product updates. Suffolk Construction named Clearstory the standard for change order management across its national portfolio in March 2026, joining Turner Construction which has deployed the platform on more than three hundred fifty projects. New AI features include AI Reason Categories, which automatically classifies change orders into root cause categories for change order intelligence; Bulk AI Import, which brings entire COR backlogs into the system with AI-driven field mapping; a new Sage Intacct integration; and Procore Contract Mapping for automatic contract data population. Specialty contractors get change orders approved up to seventy-five percent faster and cut processing time by seventy percent or more. The basic tier is free for all stakeholders.

tools.name: Bluebeam Max
tools.description: AI-enhanced version of Bluebeam Revu, launched globally May 19, 2026. Smart Review scans drawings and specs for omissions, inconsistencies, and scope gaps using AI trained on construction documents (incorporating Firmus AI acquisition technology). Smart Overlay detects design changes across disciplines and drawing scales with visual overlays and trackable comparisons. Stitching automatically combines fragmented PDF drawing sets into a continuous navigable view. Integrates with Anthropic Claude via Model Context Protocol (MCP) for natural language automation of Revu workflows, and with Autodesk Revit (markups auto-link to corresponding Revit locations). Built to be model-agnostic.
tools.pricing: $590/user/year introductory (locked through 2027 renewal); standard Bluebeam plans $260-$440/year
tools.results: Globally launched May 19, 2026 with full commercial availability. MCP integration enables natural language automation across desktop and CAD workflows.
tools.integrations: Autodesk Revit (native markup linkage), Procore, Anthropic Claude via MCP, Microsoft Copilot
tools.spoken: Bluebeam Max launched globally on May nineteenth, twenty twenty-six, adding an AI intelligence layer on top of the construction document platform used by millions of AEC professionals. Smart Review scans construction documents for design issues, scope gaps, and discrepancies. Smart Overlay detects design changes across disciplines and drawing scales, critical for pricing change order impacts. VisualSearch uses AI for quantity takeoffs, show Revu an image to automatically identify and count all other instances across drawings. It integrates with Anthropic Claude via the Model Context Protocol and with Autodesk Revit through Connected Studio Sessions. Pricing starts at five hundred ninety dollars per user per year introductory through 2027 renewal.

tools.name: Mastt
tools.description: AI-powered construction project management platform for capital project owners, project management consultants, and owner's representatives. AI Contract and Payment Review automatically extracts data from contracts and payment applications, checks compliance against contract terms, flags discrepancies and payment risks, and maintains audit-ready records. Provides live portfolio dashboards, budget forecasts, and schedule tracking. Australia-based with global project deployments.
tools.pricing: Starting approximately $150/project/month; enterprise pricing custom
tools.results: AI payment review flags contract compliance issues before they escalate into disputes. Recognized in multiple 2026 construction AI roundups for owner and PMC use cases.
tools.integrations: Major construction management platforms; API connections

tools.name: BidIntell
tools.description: AI pre-bid scoring platform that anchors proposal decisions by evaluating bid fit before any estimating labor is invested, using BidIndex scoring on location, contract terms, GC history, and trade match.
tools.pricing: $49/user/month
tools.results: Average bid preparation time industry-wide down from 34 hours to 14 hours per project for AI-using contractors (Dodge Construction Network 2025).

market_context.owner_expectations: Major general contractors and virtually all ENR Top 400 GCs now require specialty subcontractors to operate within the GC's project management platform from day one. RFIs, submittals, daily reports, pay applications, and schedule updates must flow through Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud/Build, or Kahua. Procore and Autodesk do not charge subcontractors added to a GC's instance (unlimited-user licenses cover invited subs at no additional cost), so the real investment is workflow adoption and staff training. Beyond platform compliance, GCs increasingly scrutinize cybersecurity provisions governing subcontractor use of AI tools — 2026 contracts are beginning to specify which AI tools are permitted, require notification if project data is used to train third-party models, and align subcontract AI governance language with prime contract requirements. According to an AGC 2026 Outlook survey, 23% of firms now use AI for estimating and 61% are using AI or plan to increase AI investment in 2026. AI-powered bid analysis adoption grew 340% between 2024 and 2026, signaling that subs who cannot demonstrate digital readiness risk being deprioritized in bid invitations.
market_context.adoption_cost: For a specialty subcontractor with 5-20 project managers, three adoption tiers reflect different levels of digital maturity. Compliance-only tier (responding to GC platform requirements without owning your own license): $5,000-$20,000 per year, covering Bluebeam Revu at approximately $240 per user per year, basic cloud collaboration such as Microsoft 365 or Google Workspace at $150-$360 per user per year, and essential field tools. Core PM and field stack (own Procore or Autodesk license plus field reporting and document control): $30,000-$80,000 per year for a 10-PM team, rising to $100,000-$150,000 with estimating, scheduling, and billing modules added. Full AI-integrated stack (adding AI estimating such as Togal.AI or Beam AI, AI scheduling, change order automation, and safety monitoring): $80,000-$200,000 per year in software alone, with first-year total cost including integration, data migration, and training running $100,000-$300,000 for most SME subcontractors. Point solutions offer entry at lower cost — BidIntell at $49/user/month, AI estimating tools between $149-$299/user/month, and safety AI starting around $500-$2,000/month per site — allowing phased adoption that avoids large upfront commitments. Budget an additional 20-30% of software cost annually for training, change management, and ongoing configuration.
market_context.roi: Documented ROI spans several adoption layers. Platform-level (Procore 2022 survey of 2,600+ customers, still widely cited in 2026 industry research): 48% more construction volume managed per project manager, 15 days saved on overall project schedule, RFIs completed 8 days faster, change orders approved 8 days faster, nearly half of optimized users saving 5+ hours per week. Subcontractor case studies: Green Mechanical (HVAC sub) saved $115,000 in year one with each PM recovering 4-10 hours per week. NC Painting scaled to 60 bids per month in 60 days using Togal.AI. Coastal Construction saved 14.5 hours per plan set, totaling approximately $1M in annual cost savings. SubBase AI reconciles invoices in 21 seconds versus 3-5 minutes manually. ServiceTitan's 2026 Commercial Specialty Contractor Industry Report found 38% of contractors now report measurable business impact from AI, up from 17% one year prior. Among early AI adopters, 68% have saved at least $50,000 annually, with typical savings of 500-1,000 labor hours per year. AI can reduce overall project costs by 10-15% through more accurate estimates and better schedule optimization, but typical satisfactory ROI takes 2-4 years to materialize — longer than the 7-12 month norm for conventional technology investments — due to integration complexity, workflow retraining, and data quality requirements. Electrical subcontractor estimating benchmarks show the measurement phase dropping from 2.5-3.5 hours to as little as 42 seconds with AI takeoff tools.
